perturbation.original_text: The Blockchain Administrator shall perform (or oversee, or arrange for, the performance of) development and administrative services necessary for the issuance of the Fund's shares as ERC-1404 compatible digital securities ("Shares") and the on-going maintenance and administration of the smart contracts underlying such Shares. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Blockchain Administrator shall provide the Fund with facilities, equipment, technology, coding and such other services as the Blockchain Administrator, subject to review by the Board, shall from time to time determine to be necessary or useful to perform its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Blockchain Administrator shall perform (or oversee, or arrange for, the performance of) development and administrative services necessary for the issuance of the Fund's shares as digital securities ("Shares") and the on-going maintenance and administration of the underlying contracts for such Shares. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Blockchain Administrator shall provide the Fund with facilities, equipment, technology, coding and such other services as the Blockchain Administrator, subject to review by the Board, shall from time to time determine to be necessary or useful to perform its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'ERC-1404 compatible digital securities' was removed from the definition. This creates an omission that contradicts the intent to issue specific type of digital securities. The mention of `ERC-1404` standard specifies a certain level of security and compliance that is not guaranteed anymore with the change. This omission introduces uncertainty about whether the digital securities will adhere to the ERC-1404 standard, which may affect their regulatory compliance and transferability, while the agreement still refers to digital securities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Blockchain Administrator shall also, on behalf of the Fund, conduct relations with custodians, depositories, transfer agents, dividend disbursing agents, other stockholder servicing agents, accountants, attorneys, underwriters, brokers and dealers, corporate fiduciaries, insurers, banks and such other persons as the Blockchain Administrator shall deem to be necessary or desirable in connection with the issuance and transfer of the Fund's ERC-1404 compatible digital security,.
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the term 'ERC-1404 compatible' from the definition of Shares. The contradicted text explicitly references 'the Fund's ERC-1404 compatible digital security'. By removing the ERC-1404 compatibility requirement in the definition, the modified text creates a contradiction with the contradicted text because the agreement no longer guarantees that the digital securities will be ERC-1404 compatible, making the contradicted section potentially unenforceable or unclear.

perturbation.original_text: The Blockchain Administrator agrees to maintain and keep all books, accounts and other records of the Fund that relate to activities performed by the Blockchain Administrator hereunder and will maintain and keep such books, accounts and records in accordance with the Investment Company Act. In compliance with the requirements of Rule 31a-3 under the Investment Company Act, the Blockchain Administrator agrees that all records which it maintains for the Fund shall at all times remain the property of the Fund, shall be readily accessible during normal business hours, and shall be promptly surrendered upon the termination of the Agreement or otherwise on written request.
perturbation.changed_text: The Blockchain Administrator agrees to maintain and keep all books, accounts and other records of the Fund that relate to activities performed by the Blockchain Administrator hereunder and will maintain and keep such books, accounts and records. In compliance with the requirements of Rule 31a-3, the Blockchain Administrator agrees that all records which it maintains for the Fund shall at all times remain the property of the Fund, shall be readily accessible during normal business hours, and shall be promptly surrendered upon the termination of the Agreement or otherwise on written request.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'in accordance with the Investment Company Act' was removed, and also the phrase 'under the Investment Company Act' in the next sentences. This is an omission. Although the paragraph still requires the Blockchain Administrator to keep records, it no longer explicitly states they must be kept in compliance with the Investment Company Act. This introduces ambiguity and creates an internal contradiction if the fund's operations are predicated on adhering to the Act's record-keeping requirements. This can lead to uncertainty about whether the Fund is meeting its regulatory obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Blockchain Administrator further agrees that all records which it maintains for the Fund pursuant to Rule 31a-1 under the Investment Company Act will be preserved for the periods prescribed by Rule 31a-2 under the Investment Company Act unless any such records are earlier surrendered as provided above. Records shall be surrendered in usable machine-readable form. The Blockchain Administrator shall have the right to retain copies of such records subject to observance of its confidentiality obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated adherence to the Investment Company Act for record-keeping. The changed text omits this requirement, creating a conflict with the contradicted text, which explicitly references Rule 31a-1 and Rule 31a-2 under the Investment Company Act. By removing the general obligation to comply with the Act, the change creates ambiguity about whether the record-keeping provisions related to Rules 31a-1 and 31a-2 are still applicable, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York applicable to contracts formed and to be performed entirely within the State of New York, without regard to conflict of laws principles, and in accordance with the applicable provisions of the Investment Fund Act. In such case, to the extent the applicable laws of the State of New York, or any of the provisions herein, conflict with the provisions of the Investment Fund Act, the latter shall control.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York applicable to contracts formed and to be performed entirely within the State of New York, without regard to conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'and in accordance with the applicable provisions of the Investment Fund Act. In such case, to the extent the applicable laws of the State of New York, or any of the provisions herein, conflict with the provisions of the Investment Fund Act, the latter shall control.' was removed. This omission of a clause that dictates the Investment Fund Act takes precedence over New York law in case of conflict. This creates an internal contradiction because other sections of the agreement still reference and rely on compliance with the Investment Company Act (Sections 1(g) and 2), but this section now suggests New York law has primary authority.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Blockchain Administrator is hereby authorized to enter into agreements with other service providers pursuant to which the Blockchain Administrator may obtain the services of the service providers in fulfilling its responsibilities hereunder. The Blockchain Administrator shall ensure that any such service provider shall operate in conformity with the requirements of the Investment Company Act and other applicable federal and state law and shall maintain books and records of the Fund (if any) in a manner substantially similar to Section 2 of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states that the Investment Fund Act takes precedence over New York law in case of a conflict. The changed text removes this clause, creating a direct contradiction with the contradicted text. The contradicted text mandates that the Blockchain Administrator ensures service providers operate in conformity with the Investment Company Act. By removing the clause that prioritizes the Investment Fund Act, the changed text now implies that New York law has primary authority, potentially conflicting with the requirement to comply with the Investment Company Act as stated in the contradicted text. This presents a clear contradiction that would cause confusion, unenforceability, or a legal issue within the document, because the agreement can be read as giving conflicting instructions as to which law takes precedence.
